items.prompt: A plane cuts a double-napped right circular cone (not through the vertex) of semi-vertical angle alpha. If the plane makes an angle beta with the axis where alpha < beta < 90 degrees, the section is:
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - maths-ch10 Sec 10.2.1 Circle, ellipse, parabola and hyperbola

items.prompt: Find the equation of the circle with centre (-2, 3) and radius 4.

items.prompt: The circle x^2 + y^2 + 8x + 10y - 8 = 0 is written in standard form by completing the square. Find the radius of this circle.
Find the equation of the parabola with focus (6, 0) and directrix x = -6.

items.prompt: For the ellipse x^2/25 + y^2/9 = 1, the major axis is along the x-axis. Find its eccentricity e = c/a (give the decimal value).
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - maths-ch10 Sec 10.5.2, cf. Example 9

items.prompt: For the ellipse x^2/36 + y^2/16 = 1, the latus rectum has length 2b^2/a. Compute its value as a decimal.
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - maths-ch10 Sec 10.5.4, cf. Exercise 10.3 Q1

items.prompt: For the hyperbola x^2/16 - y^2/9 = 1, the foci lie on the x-axis at (+-c, 0). Using c^2 = a^2 + b^2, find c.
items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - maths-ch10 Sec 10.6.2, cf. Exercise 10.4 Q1

items.prompt: State the geometric definition of a hyperbola, and explain how it differs from the definition of an ellipse.

expected.rubric: A hyperbola is the set of all points in a plane the DIFFERENCE of whose distances from two fixed points (the foci) is a constant. An ellipse, by contrast, is the set of points the SUM of whose distances from the two foci is a constant. So both are loci defined by the two focal distances, but the ellipse uses their sum while the hyperbola uses their difference.
markPoints: Hyperbola: difference of distances from the two foci is constant
Ellipse: sum of distances from the two foci is constant
Both are focal loci; the distinction is difference (hyperbola) versus sum (ellipse)

items.srcLabel: Authored practice (Opus) - maths-ch10 Sec 10.6 Definition 7 (vs Sec 10.5 Definition 4)

items.prompt: Find the length of the latus rectum of the hyperbola x^2/9 - y^2/16 = 1, using latus rectum = 2b^2/a. Give the decimal value.
